title: Schema-First Document Compilation Pipeline
summary: Introduce a compile pipeline where agents submit validated JSON against template schemas; Typst rendering remains an internal implementation detail.
motivation: Direct Typst authoring by agents is error-prone and hard to lint. A schema-first contract enables validation, repair loops, and consistent business document output across templates.
specification:
1. Each template ships schema.json and sample.json validated against it.
2. docforge_create_document accepts template_id + data; schema validation runs before workspace creation.
3. compile_document invokes typst CLI 0.14+ with data.json injected into the workspace.
4. On failure, diagnostics map to agent_action and suggested_repairs for automated retry.
5. Marketplace templates resolve from marketplace/{id} with the same package layout as builtins.

alternatives.title: Agent-authored Typst
alternatives.description: Let agents write main.typ directly. Rejected due to layout fragility and poor lint coverage.

alternatives.title: HTML-to-PDF via headless browser
alternatives.description: Faster iteration for web developers but weaker typography, accessibility tagging, and version pinning than Typst.

alternatives.title: LaTeX templates
alternatives.description: Mature ecosystem but slower compile times and steeper agent error surface for business documents.

unresolved_questions: Should marketplace templates require golden compile tests in CI before listing?
How do we version schema breaking changes without orphaning in-flight documents?
What is the policy for community author attribution vs. forked builtins?
